input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>

</ContentPage>
```

Actually, `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView` — here's the correct structure:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using MVVM Community Toolkit):
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Fires when SelectedItem changes — navigate to detail page
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;

        _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new() { { "Item", value } });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so the same item can be re-selected
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        IsRefreshing = true;
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(await _api.GetItemsAsync());
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Property | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | One-way | Populates the list |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` | Two-way | Triggers navigation on tap; reset to `null` after navigating so the item can be re-selected |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | Two-way | Controls the pull-to-refresh spinner |
| `Command` → `RefreshCommand` | One-way | Auto-generated by `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync` |

**Important details:**
- `RefreshView` must **wrap** the `CollectionView`, not sit inside it.
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating — otherwise tapping the same row a second time won't fire the change notification.
- `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of `RefreshAsync` dismisses the spinner.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI CollectionView example with single selection and pull-to-refresh, plus navigation to a detail page. This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/XAML development - no codebase exploration needed.
